title: internal virutalization error when enable debug
author: simplaman

title: Halt in iBoot Stage 1 and in iBoot Stage 2 don't work
author: steven-michaud
body:
Or at least I don't understand how they're supposed to work.

I tried setting either or both of them on macOS 14 Beta 2 and macOS 13.4 VMs, but those settings seemed to make no difference. Both VMs booted normally. I'm doing this on a macOS 14 Beta 2 host, with XCode 15 Beta 2 installed (plus its commandline tools).

I'm trying to get them to halt so I can use `irecovery` to feed them my own, patched `LLB.vma2.RELEASE.img4`, `iBSS.vma2.RELEASE.img4` and/or `iBoot.vma2.RELEASE.img4` files (generated from files in Apple's IPSWs), along the lines of [this document](https://github.com/NyanSatan/Virtual-iBoot-Fun).

title: Scrolling with trackpad on macOS 12 causes the entire VM to freeze
author: jonpalmisc
body:
VirtualApple version: `master` at time of writing
Host version: macOS 12.6.1 (21G217)
Guest version: macOS 13.0.1 (22A400)

Title basically says it all; attempting to perform a two-finger scroll with the trackpad causes the entire VM to hang, requiring it to be stopped and restarted.

title: GDB stub causes boot failure
author: linden
body: Currently when attempting to boot with GDB stub enabled it causes the VM to fail, without it enabled it boots fine. Full configuration. Error message. P.S. great project, thanks for building it!

title: spctl settings don't persist after reboot
author: anoane
body:
I'm trying to set spctl settings from recovery, but it seems changes are not stored after rebooting, I was thinking it AUX partition was used for this, but apparently it's not.
I'm trying for example:
spctl kext-consent disable
but on reboot 
spctl kext-consent status
tells me it is enabled again
do you have any idea/hint of what it could be?
